name: KrillApp.Executor.LogicGate
title: Logic Gates
description: Reads boolean values from one or two input nodes (Pins or DataPoints), applies a logic gate operation (AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR, BUFFER, IMPLY, NIMPLY), and stores the boolean result in its own meta.snapshot; observers pull it from there (e.g., a Pin or DataPoint that lists the gate in its sources and inputs). Pin values are read as ON=true/OFF=false. DataPoint values are read as 1.0=true/0.0=false.
shortDescription: Applies boolean logic to its input nodes' values and stores the result in its own snapshot.
llmPurpose: Evaluates a boolean logic gate on one or two input node values and stores the result in its own meta.snapshot for observers (Pin or DataPoint) to pull.
llmBehavior: invoked when an observed source completes (SOURCE_INVOKED)
reads boolean values from its input nodes
stores the boolean result in its own meta.snapshot; nodes observing the gate are invoked
supports re-entrancy protection to prevent circular invocation
llmInputs: 1-2 input nodes (Pin state or DataPoint snapshot)
llmOutputs: boolean result stored in own meta.snapshot; observers pull it (Pin reads ON/OFF, DataPoint reads 1.0/0.0)
llmTypicalUseCases: combine two sensor states with AND logic
invert a pin state with NOT gate
create conditional logic between pins and data points
llmCreationHints: Create a LogicGate when the user needs boolean logic between nodes. Requires selecting a gate type and one or two boolean inputs (Pins or DataPoints), listed in both the gate's sources (with SOURCE_INVOKED, so it re-evaluates when they change) and its inputs. The node that should react to the result (a Pin or DataPoint) observes the gate via its own sources and inputs.

llmConnectionHints.role: computation node that reads its inputs and stores a boolean result in its own snapshot
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Observer-side: list the 1-2 boolean inputs in both sources (SOURCE_INVOKED) and inputs so the gate fires when they change; the Pin or DataPoint that should apply the result observes this gate via its own sources+inputs.

llmExamples: If both sensors are ON, turn on this pin.
Invert the state of this GPIO pin.
AND these two temperature readings and record the result in a data point.
Create an XOR gate between these two inputs.
